description_long: This object is a miniature airplane with a compact, streamlined fuselage and short, straight wings extending from either side. The wings are positioned low on the body, and the tail section features a conventional horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin. The nose is rounded, and the cockpit area is subtly indicated near the front, though no detailed windows or canopy are visible. The landing gear is not discernible, suggesting it may be retracted or not modeled. The surface appears uniformly dark, with a matte or semi-gloss finish, and the overall form is simple and blocky, lacking intricate surface details.
description: A small, dark, streamlined airplane with low straight wings and a conventional tail, featuring a simple, blocky design.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from a slight angle, showing the left wing and the rounded nose most prominently.
description_view_1: A direct side view highlights the profile of the fuselage, the low-mounted wing, and the tail fin.
description_view_2: The rear angle reveals the tail section, including the horizontal stabilizer and vertical fin, with both wings visible.
description_view_3: The right side of the airplane is visible, mirroring the left, with the wing and tail fin in profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted cockpit, enclosed, with no visible canopy or window details.
